root_text.sn55.53:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā bārāṇasiyaṁ viharati isipatane migadāye.
root_text.sn55.53:1.2: Atha kho dhammadinno upāsako pañcahi upāsakasatehi saddhiṁ yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho dhammadinno upāsako bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn55.53:1.4: anusāsatu no, bhante, bhagavā yaṁ amhākaṁ assa dīgharattaṁ hitāya sukhāyā”ti.
root_text.sn55.53:2.2: ‘ye te suttantā tathāgatabhāsitā gambhīrā gambhīratthā lokuttarā suññatapaṭisaṁyuttā te kālena kālaṁ upasampajja viharissāmā’ti.
root_text.sn55.53:2.4: “Na kho netaṁ, bhante, sukaraṁ amhehi puttasambādhasayanaṁ ajjhāvasantehi kāsikacandanaṁ paccanubhontehi mālāgandhavilepanaṁ dhārayantehi jātarūparajataṁ sādiyantehi—
root_text.sn55.53:2.5: ye te suttantā tathāgatabhāsitā gambhīrā gambhīratthā lokuttarā suññatapaṭisaṁyuttā te kālena kālaṁ upasampajja viharituṁ.
root_text.sn55.53:2.6: Tesaṁ no, bhante, bhagavā amhākaṁ pañcasu sikkhāpadesu ṭhitānaṁ uttaridhammaṁ desetū”ti.
root_text.sn55.53:3.3: itipi so bhagavā …pe… satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavāti.
root_text.sn55.53:3.6: ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgatā bhavissāma akhaṇḍehi …pe… samādhisaṁvattanikehī’ti.
root_text.sn55.53:4.1: “Yānimāni, bhante, bhagavatā cattāri sotāpattiyaṅgāni desitāni, saṁvijjante dhammā amhesu, mayañca tesu dhammesu sandissāma.
root_text.sn55.53:4.3: itipi so bhagavā …pe… satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavāti.

translation_text.sn55.53:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Varanasi, in the deer park at Isipatana.
translation_text.sn55.53:1.2: Then the lay follower Dhammadinna, together with five hundred lay followers, went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.sn55.53:1.4: and instruct us. It will be for our lasting welfare and happiness.”
translation_text.sn55.53:2.1: “So, Dhammadinna, you should train like this:
translation_text.sn55.53:2.2: ‘From time to time we will undertake and dwell upon the discourses spoken by the Realized One that are deep, profound, transcendent, dealing with emptiness.’
translation_text.sn55.53:2.3: That’s how you should train yourselves.”
translation_text.sn55.53:2.4: “Sir, we live at home with our children, using sandalwood imported from Kāsi, wearing garlands, fragrance, and makeup, and accepting gold and currency.
translation_text.sn55.53:2.5: It’s not easy for us to undertake and dwell from time to time upon the discourses spoken by the Realized One that are deep, profound, transcendent, dealing with emptiness.
translation_text.sn55.53:2.6: Since we are established in the five training rules, please teach us further.”
translation_text.sn55.53:3.1: “So, Dhammadinna, you should train like this:
translation_text.sn55.53:3.2: ‘We will have experiential confidence in the Buddha …
translation_text.sn55.53:3.6: And we will have the ethical conduct loved by the noble ones … leading to immersion.’
translation_text.sn55.53:3.7: That’s how you should train yourselves.”
translation_text.sn55.53:4.1: “Sir, these four factors of stream-entry that were taught by the Buddha are found in us, and we embody them.
translation_text.sn55.53:4.2: For we have experiential confidence in the Buddha …
translation_text.sn55.53:4.6: And we have the ethical conduct loved by the noble ones … leading to immersion.”
translation_text.sn55.53:4.8: You have all declared the fruit of stream-entry.”
